description: Fix five bugs in the Chrome extension: missing throttle cleanup on debugger detach, silent error swallowing in failed-plugin removal, incorrect optimistic-update rollback in BrowserToolsCard for concurrent failures, dangling message ports from handlePluginReadinessChanged, and alwaysAllow state leaking across ConfirmationDialog navigation.

userStories.title: Call cleanupThrottle in chrome.debugger.onDetach handler
userStories.description: The chrome.debugger.onDetach listener in network-capture.ts omits cleanupThrottle(tabId), leaving a stale entry in the throttlingTabs Set. This causes subsequent browser_throttle_network calls on the same tab to fail because ensureDebuggerAttached in throttle-commands.ts sees isThrottling(tabId) === true and skips re-attaching, then the CDP command fails with a 'Debugger not attached' error.
acceptanceCriteria: chrome.debugger.onDetach listener calls cleanupThrottle(tabId) alongside cleanupInterception and cleanupEmulation
cleanupThrottle is called before captures.delete(tabId) so the throttle state is always cleared when the debugger detaches, regardless of whether network capture was active

userStories.title: Show error feedback when handleRemoveFailedPlugin fails
userStories.description: handleRemoveFailedPlugin in App.tsx silently swallows server errors: the spinner disappears but no error message is shown. Every other action handler (handleRemove, handleUpdate, handleInstall) shows an error. Fix the .catch handler to give the user feedback when failed-plugin removal fails.
acceptanceCriteria: When removeFailedPlugin() rejects, the user sees an error message — either in the FailedPluginCard itself or via a fallback notification mechanism
The catch handler still clears the loading spinner (removes specifier from removingFailedPlugins) even when showing an error

userStories.title: Fix BrowserToolsCard tool permission rollback for concurrent failures
userStories.description: BrowserToolsCard.handleToolPermissionChange uses a single preToggleRef to store the pre-change tools array. If two tool permissions are changed in rapid succession and both requests fail, only the last failure's rollback fires, leaving the first change's optimistic update stuck in the UI. PluginCard solves this correctly with a Map of per-version rollback states — apply the same pattern to BrowserToolsCard.
acceptanceCriteria: Each pending tool permission change stores its own pre-change rollback data independently (keyed by version number, not overwriting a shared ref)
When a setToolPermission request fails, only that specific tool's change is reverted in the UI — other pending changes are not affected
The rollback entry is cleaned up (deleted from the Map) in .finally() regardless of success or failure

userStories.title: Call sendResponse in handlePluginReadinessChanged to release message port
userStories.description: handlePluginReadinessChanged never calls sendResponse, but the background message listener returns true (async) for all registered handlers. Chrome keeps the message port open for several minutes per event until GC collects the unreferenced sendResponse. Since readiness-changed events fire on auth state changes, dangling ports accumulate during active sessions. Fix: call sendResponse({ ok: true }) immediately (fire-and-forget) before the async work begins.
acceptanceCriteria: handlePluginReadinessChanged calls sendResponse (renamed from _sendResponse) with { ok: true } immediately — not after the async IIFE completes
The async plugin readiness probe still runs in the background; the sendResponse call does not block or skip it

userStories.title: Reset alwaysAllow when navigating between pending confirmations
userStories.description: ConfirmationDialog.tsx does not reset the alwaysAllow switch when the user navigates between confirmations using the prev/next buttons. If a user checks 'Always allow' while viewing confirmation A, then navigates to confirmation B and clicks 'Allow', tool B receives auto-permission even though the user only intended one-time permission. Fix: reset alwaysAllow to false on every navigation.
acceptanceCriteria: Clicking the 'prev' button resets alwaysAllow to false so the switch is unchecked on the newly visible confirmation
Clicking the 'next' button resets alwaysAllow to false so the switch is unchecked on the newly visible confirmation
alwaysAllow is still correctly reset to false in handleAllow and handleDeny (existing behavior preserved)
userStories.notes:
Files:
- platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/ConfirmationDialog.tsx (add setAlwaysAllow(false) in prev/next onClick handlers)

Context:
Current prev/next button onClick handlers (approximately lines 93-106):
```
<button
  type="button"
  ...
  disabled={safeIndex === 0}
  onClick={() => setCurrentIndex(i => i - 1)}>
  prev
</button>
<button
  type="button"
  ...
  disabled={safeIndex >= count - 1}
  onClick={() => setCurrentIndex(i => i + 1)}>
  next
</button>
```

Fix — update both onClick handlers to also reset alwaysAllow:
```
onClick={() => { setCurrentIndex(i => i - 1); setAlwaysAllow(false); }}
```
```
onClick={() => { setCurrentIndex(i => i + 1); setAlwaysAllow(false); }}
```

Constraints:
- Do NOT change any other behavior in ConfirmationDialog
- Do NOT change handleAllow or handleDeny — they already reset alwaysAllow correctly
- This is a two-line change (one per button)
